metadata.description: Challenges validating resilience patterns in network protocol services: circuit breaker state transitions, connection limiter saturation, timeout handling, reconnection behavior, concurrent operation limits, error cascading prevention, and graceful degradation under failure conditions

challenges.name: Circuit Breaker: CLOSED to OPEN Transition
challenges.description: Verify that CircuitBreaker transitions from CLOSED to OPEN state after the configured failure threshold is reached. Send exactly failureThreshold consecutive failing requests and verify the circuit opens. Verify that the (failureThreshold - 1)th failure keeps the circuit closed while the failureThreshold-th failure opens it.

outputs.name: state_before_threshold
outputs.description: Circuit state after (threshold - 1) failures

outputs.name: state_at_threshold
outputs.description: Circuit state after exactly threshold failures

outputs.name: transition_correct
outputs.description: Whether the transition occurred at the correct failure count

assertions.message: Circuit must remain CLOSED before failure threshold is reached
Circuit must transition to OPEN at failure threshold
Transition must occur at exactly the configured threshold

challenges.name: Circuit Breaker: Request Rejection While OPEN
challenges.description: Verify that CircuitBreaker in OPEN state immediately rejects all incoming requests without forwarding them to the underlying service. After opening the circuit, send 100 requests and verify all are rejected with CircuitBreakerOpenException. Measure rejection latency to ensure it is near-zero (no network call made).

outputs.name: rejected_count
outputs.description: Number of requests rejected while circuit is open

outputs.name: forwarded_count
outputs.description: Number of requests forwarded to underlying service

outputs.name: avg_rejection_latency_ms
outputs.description: Average latency for rejected requests

assertions.message: No request may be forwarded while circuit is OPEN
All 100 requests must be rejected
Rejection latency must be near-zero (under 5ms)

challenges.name: Circuit Breaker: OPEN to HALF_OPEN Transition
challenges.description: Verify that CircuitBreaker transitions from OPEN to HALF_OPEN after the configured reset timeout expires. After opening the circuit, wait for resetTimeout duration and verify the state changes to HALF_OPEN. Then send one successful request and verify the circuit transitions back to CLOSED.

outputs.name: state_before_timeout
outputs.description: Circuit state before reset timeout expires

outputs.name: state_after_timeout
outputs.description: Circuit state after reset timeout expires

outputs.name: state_after_success
outputs.description: Circuit state after successful request in HALF_OPEN

outputs.name: full_cycle_completed
outputs.description: Whether CLOSED -> OPEN -> HALF_OPEN -> CLOSED cycle completed

assertions.message: Circuit must remain OPEN before reset timeout
Circuit must transition to HALF_OPEN after reset timeout
Circuit must return to CLOSED after successful HALF_OPEN request
Full circuit breaker lifecycle must complete

challenges.name: Circuit Breaker: HALF_OPEN Failure Reopens Circuit
challenges.description: Verify that a failed request during HALF_OPEN state immediately returns the circuit to OPEN state. After the circuit transitions to HALF_OPEN, send a failing request and verify the circuit reopens. This prevents premature recovery when the underlying service is still unhealthy.

outputs.name: state_after_half_open_failure
outputs.description: Circuit state after failure in HALF_OPEN

outputs.name: reopen_immediate
outputs.description: Whether the circuit reopened immediately on HALF_OPEN failure

assertions.message: Circuit must return to OPEN after HALF_OPEN failure
Reopening must be immediate on HALF_OPEN failure

challenges.name: Connection Limiter: Saturation Behavior
challenges.description: Verify that ConnectionLimiter correctly limits concurrent connections to the configured maximum. Start maxConnections simultaneous operations, then attempt one more and verify it either queues or is rejected. After one operation completes, verify the queued operation proceeds.

outputs.name: concurrent_at_saturation
outputs.description: Number of concurrent operations at saturation

outputs.name: overflow_behavior
outputs.description: How overflow operation was handled (queued/rejected)

outputs.name: overflow_completed_after_release
outputs.description: Whether overflow operation completed after a slot was released

assertions.message: Concurrent operations at saturation must equal max_connections
Overflow operation must complete after a connection slot is released

challenges.name: Connection Limiter: Non-Blocking Semaphore
challenges.description: Verify that ConnectionLimiter uses a non-blocking semaphore implementation. When the limiter is saturated, new acquire attempts must not block the calling thread but instead suspend the coroutine. Verify by running on a single-threaded dispatcher and confirming no deadlock occurs.

outputs.name: deadlock_detected
outputs.description: Whether a deadlock was detected on single-threaded dispatcher

outputs.name: all_operations_completed
outputs.description: Whether all operations completed successfully

assertions.message: No deadlock may occur with non-blocking semaphore
All operations must complete even on single-threaded dispatcher

challenges.name: Connection Timeout Enforcement
challenges.description: Verify that all protocol services enforce connection timeouts. Configure a 5-second connection timeout, then attempt to connect to an unreachable host. The connection attempt must fail within the timeout window (5 seconds + tolerance) rather than hanging indefinitely. Test across FTP, SFTP, WebDAV, SMB, and S3 services.

outputs.name: timeout_results
outputs.description: Connection timeout results per protocol

outputs.name: hanging_protocols
outputs.description: Protocols that exceeded the timeout window

outputs.name: all_timed_out_correctly
outputs.description: Whether all protocols timed out within the configured window

assertions.message: No protocol may hang beyond the configured connection timeout
All protocols must enforce connection timeout
All timeout results must show correct enforcement

challenges.name: Request Timeout Enforcement
challenges.description: Verify that protocol services enforce request-level timeouts independent of connection timeouts. Establish a connection successfully, then send a request to an endpoint that intentionally delays response. The request must fail within the configured request timeout.

outputs.name: request_timeout_results
outputs.description: Request timeout results per protocol

outputs.name: exceeded_timeout
outputs.description: Protocols where request exceeded timeout

assertions.message: No protocol may exceed the configured request timeout
All request timeout results must show correct enforcement

challenges.name: Reconnection After Graceful Disconnect
challenges.description: Verify that protocol services can successfully reconnect after a graceful disconnect. For each protocol, connect, perform an operation, disconnect, then reconnect and perform another operation. Both operations must succeed and the service must reach CONNECTED state after reconnection.

outputs.name: reconnection_results
outputs.description: Reconnection results per protocol

outputs.name: reconnection_failed
outputs.description: Protocols that failed to reconnect

outputs.name: state_after_reconnect
outputs.description: Service state after reconnection per protocol

assertions.message: All protocols must successfully reconnect after disconnect
All reconnection sequences must succeed

challenges.name: Reconnection After Connection Error
challenges.description: Verify that protocol services can recover from connection errors by reconnecting. Simulate a connection drop (network error, server crash) during an active operation, then verify the service detects the error, transitions to DISCONNECTED/ERROR state, and can be reconnected.

outputs.name: recovery_results
outputs.description: Error recovery results per protocol

outputs.name: unrecoverable_errors
outputs.description: Errors from which the service could not recover

outputs.name: state_transitions
outputs.description: State transition log during error and recovery

assertions.message: All connection errors must be recoverable via reconnection
All error recovery sequences must succeed

challenges.name: Concurrent Operation Limits per Protocol
challenges.description: Verify that each protocol service correctly limits concurrent operations to prevent overwhelming the remote server. Launch 50 simultaneous file listing operations against a single protocol and verify that no more than the configured maximum run concurrently. Verify all operations eventually complete.

outputs.name: max_observed_concurrent
outputs.description: Maximum observed concurrent operations per protocol

outputs.name: limit_exceeded
outputs.description: Protocols where concurrent limit was exceeded

outputs.name: all_operations_completed
outputs.description: Whether all operations eventually completed

assertions.message: No protocol may exceed its concurrent operation limit
All operations must eventually complete

challenges.name: Error Cascading Prevention Across Protocols
challenges.description: Verify that a failure in one protocol service does not cascade to other protocol services. Simultaneously connect to multiple protocols, cause one to fail (e.g., Dropbox authentication failure), and verify that other protocols (Google Drive, OneDrive, FTP) continue operating normally without errors.

outputs.name: failing_protocol_state
outputs.description: State of the intentionally failed protocol

outputs.name: healthy_protocol_states
outputs.description: States of protocols that should remain healthy

outputs.name: cascade_detected
outputs.description: Whether error cascaded from failing to healthy protocols

assertions.message: Errors must not cascade between protocol services
All healthy protocols must remain operational

challenges.name: Error Cascading Prevention in Shared Resources
challenges.description: Verify that shared resources (DocumentCache, ConnectionLimiter, FormatRegistry) do not become corrupted when one protocol service fails. After a protocol failure, verify that cache lookups still work, the connection limiter still accepts new connections, and format detection still functions.

outputs.name: cache_functional
outputs.description: Whether DocumentCache remains functional after protocol failure

outputs.name: limiter_functional
outputs.description: Whether ConnectionLimiter remains functional after protocol failure

outputs.name: registry_functional
outputs.description: Whether FormatRegistry remains functional after protocol failure

outputs.name: shared_resource_corruption
outputs.description: Any shared resource corruption detected

assertions.message: DocumentCache must remain functional after protocol failure
ConnectionLimiter must remain functional after protocol failure
FormatRegistry must remain functional after protocol failure
No shared resource corruption may occur

challenges.name: Rapid Connect/Disconnect Cycle Stability
challenges.description: Verify that protocol services remain stable during rapid connect/disconnect cycles. Execute 100 consecutive connect/disconnect cycles for each cloud protocol (Dropbox, GoogleDrive, OneDrive) and verify no resource leaks (coroutine, connection, memory), no deadlocks, and the service remains usable after the final cycle.

outputs.name: cycle_results
outputs.description: Connect/disconnect cycle results per protocol

outputs.name: resource_leaks
outputs.description: Resource leaks detected during rapid cycling

outputs.name: deadlocks_detected
outputs.description: Whether any deadlocks were detected

outputs.name: final_state_usable
outputs.description: Whether the service is usable after all cycles

assertions.message: No resource leaks during rapid connect/disconnect cycles
No deadlocks during rapid connect/disconnect cycles
Service must be usable after rapid connect/disconnect cycles

challenges.name: DocumentCache LRU Eviction Under Memory Pressure
challenges.description: Verify that DocumentCache correctly evicts least-recently-used entries when the cache reaches its size limit. Fill the cache beyond capacity, then verify that the oldest entries were evicted while recently accessed entries remain. Also verify that hit/miss metrics are accurate throughout.

outputs.name: eviction_results
outputs.description: LRU eviction behavior results

outputs.name: oldest_entries_evicted
outputs.description: Whether oldest entries were evicted first

outputs.name: recent_entries_retained
outputs.description: Whether recently accessed entries were retained

outputs.name: metrics_accurate
outputs.description: Whether hit/miss metrics matched actual behavior

assertions.message: Oldest entries must be evicted first under LRU policy
Recently accessed entries must be retained
Cache hit/miss metrics must be accurate
